process_generation_workflow.overview: This section extends the BAW Blueprint Parser mode to extract and generate business process definitions from blueprint documents. Processes are initially generated as Mermaid diagrams in Markdown format, with the long-term goal of generating BPMN (BAW artifacts) for direct deployment.

core_principles.principle: Extract process flows from blueprint documents alongside business objects
Identify process steps, decision points, and swimlanes from business context
Generate human-readable Mermaid diagrams for immediate visualization
Structure process definitions to facilitate future BPMN generation
Link processes to relevant business objects for data flow understanding

step.title: Identify Process Workflows in Blueprint
step.description: Scan blueprint documents for process descriptions, workflows, and business flows

actions.action: Look for sections describing workflows, procedures, or business processes
Identify process names and high-level objectives
Extract process steps, activities, and decision points
Identify actors, roles, or swimlanes involved in processes
Note integration points and system interactions

indicators.indicator: Sequential descriptions with steps or phases
Decision trees or conditional logic descriptions

step.title: Model Process Structure
step.description: Transform extracted process information into structured process models

actions.action: Define process start and end events
Identify sequential activities and their order
Map decision points (gateways) and their conditions
Identify parallel activities or concurrent flows
Define swimlanes for different actors or systems
Link activities to relevant business objects (data inputs/outputs)

process_elements.element: Work to be performed (user task, service task, etc.)
Actor or role responsible for activities

step.title: Generate Mermaid Process Diagrams
step.description: Create Mermaid flowchart or sequence diagrams representing the processes

actions.action: Generate Mermaid syntax for each identified process
Use appropriate Mermaid diagram types (flowchart, sequence, state)
Include decision points with conditional branches
Add swimlanes for multi-actor processes
Annotate activities with business object references
Include notes for complex logic or business rules

pattern.description: Best for sequential processes with decision points
pattern.syntax:
```mermaid flowchart TD Start([Start: Application Submission]) Submit[Submit Application] Validate{Validate
Application} Review[Underwriter Review] Decision{Underwriting
Decision} Approve[Issue Policy] Decline[Send Decline Letter] End([End]) Start --> Submit Submit --> Validate Validate -->|Valid| Review Validate -->|Invalid| End Review --> Decision Decision -->|Approved| Approve Decision -->|Declined| Decline Approve --> End Decline --> End ```

pattern.description: Best for processes with multiple actors/roles
pattern.syntax:
```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Agent A1[Collect Application] A2[Submit to System] end
subgraph System S1[Validate Data] S2[Route to Underwriter] end
subgraph Underwriter U1[Review Application] U2[Make Decision] end
A1 --> A2 A2 --> S1 S1 --> S2 S2 --> U1 U1 --> U2 ```

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Agent participant System participant Underwriter participant Policyholder
Agent->>System: Submit Application System->>System: Validate Data System->>Underwriter: Route for Review Underwriter->>System: Request Medical Exam System->>Policyholder: Schedule Exam Policyholder->>System: Complete Exam System->>Underwriter: Provide Results Underwriter->>System: Approve Policy System->>Agent: Issue Policy ```

step.title: Document Process Metadata
step.description: Create structured metadata for each process to support future BPMN generation

actions.action: Document process name, description, and business purpose
List all activities with their types (user task, service task, etc.)
Define decision logic and gateway conditions
Map business objects to process activities (inputs/outputs)
Identify integration points and external systems
Note business rules and validation requirements

step.title: Generate Process Documentation
step.description: Create comprehensive Markdown documentation for each process

actions.action: Create process files under business-processes/[context]/
Include Mermaid diagrams in Markdown format
Document process steps with descriptions
Link to related business objects
Include business rules and decision criteria
Add process metadata in structured format

step.title: Create Process Catalog
step.description: Generate a catalog of all processes for the business context

actions.action: Create process catalog at business-processes/catalog/[context].process-catalog.json
List all processes with metadata
Include relationships between processes
Link processes to business objects they use
Maintain master process catalog

step.catalog_structure: { "context": "LifeInsuranceAndAnnuities", "version": "1.0.0", "generatedDate": "2026-05-06T10:00:00Z", "processes": [ { "processId": "new-business-application", "processName": "New Business Application Process", "description": "End-to-end process for new policy applications", "processFile": "business-processes/LifeInsuranceAndAnnuities/NewBusinessApplication.process.md", "actors": ["Agent", "System", "Underwriter", "Medical Examiner"], "businessObjects": ["Application", "Policy", "UnderwritingDecision"], "subProcesses": ["medical-underwriting", "policy-issuance"], "complexity": "high", "estimatedDuration": "2-4 weeks" } ] }

type.name: end_to_end_process
type.description: Complete business process from start to finish
type.example: New Business Application Process, Claims Processing

type.name: subprocess
type.description: Reusable process component called by parent processes

future_bpmn_preparation.overview: While current implementation generates Mermaid diagrams, the structure is designed to facilitate future BPMN generation for direct BAW deployment.

mapping.to: BPMN User Task or Service Task

preparation_steps.step: Store process metadata in JSON format for programmatic access
Use consistent naming conventions aligned with BPMN standards
Document activity types (user task, service task, script task)
Define data associations between activities and business objects
Capture gateway conditions in structured format
Maintain traceability from blueprint to process definition

file_naming.convention: Use PascalCase for process file names

integration_with_business_objects.principle: Processes and business objects are complementary artifacts

linkage.link: Process activities reference business objects as inputs/outputs
Business object lifecycle events can trigger processes
Process documentation includes business object data flow
Catalogs cross-reference processes and business objects
